message:
Welcome to Once Upon a Time in Production, the responsibility is on you to size up the problem.

*Access game links by pressing the Uptime Labs Logo in the top right of the screen*
*Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin
Hey Everyone just in, customers are reporting the site images are not appearing, is this true?.
Hello everyone
Hey, I'm here—let me know what you need me to check.
bob Iam checking on this and taking it from here as IC
:star: Take command
Thanks, let me know if you need any customer impact details or recent support tickets.

just checked I can see images are loading for me
do you see is ithis happening for any specifc customers or is it for globally?
checking reports, let me get back to you on what customers are saying
:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
:star: Attempt to replicate the issue
From what we can tell, the site isn't fully down. Customers can load pages and navigate categories, but something feels off visually
A few people mentioned pages look "incomplete" or "not finished loading"
Dashboards are showing a drop in order
:star: Use metrics as evidence
double checking reports....
Getting over 100 reports so far on this
Looks like the common thread is that product images are not loading, and customers are seeing broken image icons instead. This is consistent across browsers
Definitely a global issue

do you have scrsshoshots or error messages to check this furtehr
I am reviewing the changes which got deployed recently
Checking recent changes...
:star: Reviewed changes
:star: Facts over opinion
I did deploy a chaos test for ProductCatalogueService earlier.
But that's UAT only — nothing in prod.
Also added some extra logging in checkout.
Just log fields, shouldn't affect performance.
I pushed a small frontend UI tweak earlier.
Loading animation + accessibility stuff.
No backend or infra changes.
daniel shay Can you please review this changes which got deployed and confirm if y9ou are seeing anything first and last deployed
Checking application logs now…
Seeing a huge spike in requests hitting ProductCatalogueService.
WARN [ProductCatalogueService] Request timed out after 3000ms path=/productCatalogue method=GET
Seeing slow dependency calls too.
WARN [ProductCatalogueService] Slow response from downstream dependency latency=1800ms
PCS pod CPU is extremely high right now.
Service isn't crashing, but it's clearly struggling to keep up.

Hello everyone,

Received the messages from CS team mentined website is not working as expecetd and csutomers are complain the same, I am checking further to see whats the impact. since I am not able to replicate this on my lcoally
Which regions are affected and how many customers are impacted right now?
:star: Qualify the problem
daniel was this due to the above change?
The chaos test was only enabled in UAT, not production, so it shouldn't be affecting live traffic.
I belive, its not run wrongly to target prod config? can you please double check on this
Double-checking chaos test config now...
Confirmed: chaos test is only targeting UAT, not production.
if its matching the timimg and config
I can confirm from the deployment dashboard that the chaos test config was not applied to production; only UAT was affected.
thanks tanya do y9ou see anything on platform side ?
Checking infra now, give me a moment.
Seeing a lot more traffic reaching backend services than usual.
PCS request rate is way above baseline.
Been doing a comparison with Checkout and Frontend.
And they seem fine, let me show you
https://i.ibb.co/fVY8zWQj/Checkout.png
https://i.ibb.co/Kk5gHqt/Frontend.png

daniel CHG89420
can you pelase check this change as well to see if its related?
https://i.ibb.co/chz2PJSH/PCS.png
PCS is the clear outlier.
Might be worth looping in Security to rule out something external.
CHG89420 was the checkout service logging update, which only added more detailed payment logs and didn't touch image delivery or product catalogue endpoints.
I don't see any connection between that change and the missing images or degraded storefront experience, but happy to dig deeper if you want.
thanks
Let me know if you need any more platform-level checks or want to review deployment timelines in Grafana.
shay anything on the frontend logs?
Frontend logs show a spike in 504 Gateway Timeout errors when trying to load product images.
The errors started around the same time PCS traffic spiked.
do you see any any unusal triffic tanya compared to yeserday at this time?
Yes, PCS traffic is about 8x higher than this time yesterday, while other services are flat.
I will pull the securtity team
Looking at the alerts now.
let me raise a P1 incident
Seeing a big spike hitting image-related endpoints.
WAF rate-limit rules are firing.
ALERT: image-endpoint-rate-limit threshold=4,000/5min observed=15,000 action=BLOCK
ALERT: anomalous-request-rate endpoint=/productCatalogue status=triggered
I'm calling this for now — looks like we're under a DDoS attack.
Before we start mitigation please update the business
Please let the business know we're under a DDoS attack and I'll prepare mitigation steps.
its impacted gloably we are seeing the x traffic extra and causing the isue on PCS servie
How many customers or orders are currently affected and are we losing revenue at this moment?
*Incident Number INC1055:* Website is down due to the more taraffic across the platform
What can we do to stop the attack?
tanya thanks I will let them know it
Would you like me to advise on mitigation steps now?
Just recived the updated form security team its related to dDos attached, checking furher to migtate this issue
yes please
Creating a list of the most active IP addresses now..
Alright ive starting blocking, i'll send over a list now
Blocked IP addresses:
Acknowledged on the incident ticket, but I still need exact numbers on affected customers and lost revenue.
I've blocked several high-volume IPs at the edge.
provide the steps and see if we can block these IP quickly on your end tanya
I think that should be it, hows the platform now?
provide the steps and see if we can block these IP quickly on your end maya
:star: Form an effective investigation team
Checking if the traffic is legit...
Blocking these IPs won't help because they're real customers whose requests are being amplified by failures in the image delivery system.
bob  can you see any recovery on your sided?
So the IPs don't show typical attack patterns
What I see is legitimate user traffic behaving badly probably because something upstream isn't working as expected. The WAF is reacting to the volume, not intent.
The traffic seems legit
Im now confident that this is not a DDOS attack
Sorry for the delay, i just got in can you fill me in on whats going on?
Can you clarify what is causing the PCS to struggle, what is it getting requests for?
ok do you see any other pattern maya
No classical attack patterns; it's mostly image requests from real users being retried due to failures.
hamed the website is struggling to load the images and which is hammering the PCS service
Wait images are served from S3 through CloudFront. PCS was the old path for images
Oh thats right i forgot PCS just returns the image URLs.
Yes thats right :cold_face: we moved images out of PCS a while back.
If images aren't coming from S3, that could explain the load on PCS.
Worth checking S3 / CloudFront access.
tanya is this somthing you can check it quickly?
Checking S3 / CloudFront access now.
I think I found something.
Image requests at the edge are failing.
ERROR Static asset request failed / status=403 / reason=ExpiredPolicy
CloudFront is rejecting the image requests.
Looks like some kind of access policy or signing configuration has expired.
I'm not sure yet which part of the CloudFront setup is causing it.
When the edge can't serve images, the frontend falls back through ProductCatalogueService.
PCS was never scaled to handle that much traffic.
That explains the traffic spike and the WAF alerts.
This is not related to Ddos attchec further inverstaigate suggested that one PCS service is pointing to old path and checkiong further now what cause this change
:star: Check changes done to the platform
I need to know how many customers are unable to place orders and if we are currently losing sales.
tanya do yoiu have list of changes done in platform?
No platform changes in the last 24 hours that would affect S3 or CloudFront.
which caused this issue
Alright, thinking through fixes… give me a second.
I see two paths we could take.
Option 1: scale up ProductCatalogueService so it can absorb the fallback image traffic.
anyting expried cert or keys etc when its getthing the images?
That would stabilise the platform while images are still failing through CloudFront.
Option 2: restore the CloudFront access policy so images start loading from S3 again.
That fixes the root cause and removes the load from PCS.
Worth discussing before we commit.
Yes, the CloudFront signed URL policy expired, so image requests are being rejected.
hamed  which is quick one we can do it? option1 who can action this?
:star: Manage actions and timings
Let's quickly weigh the options.
Scaling PCS is fast, but it's just a workaround.
Fixing the policy addresses the real issue.
But that depends on AWS timing.
If we scale PCS, it means we are in no rush for renew the access policy.
So it's speed vs the proper fix.
I can scale PCS right now; it should take about 3 minutes to implement.

lets do this.
add more cap to PCS daniel 
and whoc can address option 2

lets do this.
add more cap to PCS daniel 
and whoc can address option 2?
Alright, if we go the scale route, here's the plan.
Alright, let's scale PCS for now. Post updates here.
First increase PCS replica count.
Scaling PCS replicas now.
Then raise CPU and memory limits so autoscaling has headroom.
We'll monitor latency and error rates as traffic shifts.
I'll also adjust the WAF so legitimate traffic isn't blocked.
Scaling PCS replicas now to match frontend traffic levels.
to migatig this issue we are scaling the PCS service first
Replica rollout complete.
Increasing CPU and memory limits.
bez  I wil get the reprt on this in 10mins
:star: Track actions and time box them
Limits updated.
Easing WAF rate limits so legitimate traffic can pass.
bob can you please provide me the update how many cusotmers are imapcted and revene loss for this issue?
bob can you please provide me the update how many cusotmers are imapcted and revene loss for this issue in next 5mins thanks
Done.
PCS latency is starting to improve.
Good sign. Let's keep monitoring.
Understood, please update me as soon as you have the numbers.
We're seeing about 30% of active sessions affected, with an estimated revenue loss of $12,000 so far.
I'll gather the latest customer impact and revenue loss figures and report back within 5 minutes.
thanks please udpate it
PCS error rates have dropped to normal and latency is back within SLA.
ok hamed shall we do the option 2 later once its cool down?
Yes, let's revisit option 2 after the platform is fully stable and customer impact is resolved.
bob can you see if its recovering on yoiur side?
I'll check the platform
The images are appearing again for me
Images are visible for me!!
We're seeing about 30% of active sessions affected, with an estimated revenue loss of $12,000 so far
Thank you for the update—what is the ETA for full service restoration?
Update : website is reovered now and images are loading after the scale out on PCS service
:star: Confirm resolution with the business
Thanks everyone for quick action on this
Great teamwork—site looks healthy again, nice job everyone!
I have updated the business coms
I have updated the business comms
Thanks for the update—platform metrics remain stable and no new errors are coming in.
Thanks for keeping everyone informed—no new customer complaints since the fix.
bob
Customer reports are easing and the images are loading again across the site.
Great work everyone
Indeed, for a while I was worried it was a security issue
Turns out it was a false alarm, not something you see every day
I remember Jamie had a similar issue not too long ago
